retrieved.title: Web services
retrieved.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai has a modular structure. Not every module offers the same functions (e.g., uploads or Internet access). Depending on the task, you must select the appropriate module:

- Chatbot: General chat function with image analysis and internet search capabilities
- Image generator: Tool for generating images from text descriptions
- Translator: Translating text and files in the same layout
- Document analysis: Tool for analyzing written content from documents and option for Internet research
- Laboratory: Experimental environment for testing functions and models

#

| Chatbot | Document analysis | Laboratory | Image generator | Translator |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Upload images and screenshots in chat to chat about image content | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files | No uploading of documents | Prompt limited to 4000 characters Created images cannot be customized by entering additional descriptions | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (max 40 MB per file, max 250 MB total) |
| Internet research | Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research |
| No uploading of documents | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of reference images | No uploading of images |

# Student Space: room for students

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

© Alexander / Arnberger

© derknopfdruecker.com

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

The [opening hours of the respective locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/opening-hours-and-locations) apply also to the student spaces.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.

At the University of Vienna, you do not get a ready-made schedule, telling you what courses to complete each semester. Rather, you have to plan your semester yourself.
The most important tools for doing so are the student platform u:space and the course directory u:find.

[Information about u:space and u:find](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/)

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
To access u:ai, you need your u:account. Access is via [univie.academic-ai.at](https://univie.academic-ai.at)

You have a monthly credit of €20.00 available for queries. The cost of a specific query depends on the length of the question and the length of the answer (file uploads cost more than text queries, shorter texts less than long texts). You can view and monitor your credit balance directly in u:ai. Once the amount has been used up, you will not be able to use u:ai again until the following month.

Since the monthly credit is limited, u:ai should be used specifically for study and university-related questions. The credit is automatically recharged on the first day of each month. Unused credit cannot be carried over to the following month.

**Knowledge Cut-Off:**

U:ai is currently based on information that was available until October 2024. The system does not know anything that happened after that. To incorporate up-to-date information, you must activate the [Internet search](#c1268815).

retrieved.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

If you cannot find any points in u:space, register for the course and you will see that a new points account has been created for the current semester. The allocation of points can still be changed within the registration deadline. You cannot save points for the following semester, which means that you will lose points that you have not used. The directorate of studies can add additional regulations to the point allocation system: Bonus points: If certain criteria apply (e.g. academic progress or number of ECTS credits obtained in the previous semester), students automatically receive additional points. Points of the previous semester: If you are on the waiting list of a course, you will automatically be credited the allocated points for the same course in the following semester and these will be added to your newly allocated points. You can view these extra points in the section “points of previous semester”. This only applies to allocated points. Any points that you did not allocate are lost. After the end of the registration period and the subsequent allocation of places, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail-address informing you about the status of your registration. reranked.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
u:ai has a modular structure. Not every module offers the same functions (e.g., uploads or Internet access). Depending on the task, you must select the appropriate module:

- Chatbot: General chat function with image analysis and internet search capabilities
- Image generator: Tool for generating images from text descriptions
- Translator: Translating text and files in the same layout
- Document analysis: Tool for analyzing written content from documents and option for Internet research
- Laboratory: Experimental environment for testing functions and models

#

| Chatbot | Document analysis | Laboratory | Image generator | Translator |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Upload images and screenshots in chat to chat about image content | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files | No uploading of documents | Prompt limited to 4000 characters Created images cannot be customized by entering additional descriptions | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (max 40 MB per file, max 250 MB total) |
| Internet research | Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research |
| No uploading of documents | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of reference images | No uploading of images |

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

The [opening hours of the respective locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/opening-hours-and-locations) apply also to the student spaces.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

reranked.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

reranked.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.

To access u:ai, you need your u:account. Access is via [univie.academic-ai.at](https://univie.academic-ai.at)

You have a monthly credit of €20.00 available for queries. The cost of a specific query depends on the length of the question and the length of the answer (file uploads cost more than text queries, shorter texts less than long texts). You can view and monitor your credit balance directly in u:ai. Once the amount has been used up, you will not be able to use u:ai again until the following month.

Since the monthly credit is limited, u:ai should be used specifically for study and university-related questions. The credit is automatically recharged on the first day of each month. Unused credit cannot be carried over to the following month.

**Knowledge Cut-Off:**

U:ai is currently based on information that was available until October 2024. The system does not know anything that happened after that. To incorporate up-to-date information, you must activate the [Internet search](#c1268815).

reranked.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

The allocation of points can still be changed within the registration deadline. You cannot save points for the following semester, which means that you will lose points that you have not used. The directorate of studies can add additional regulations to the point allocation system: Bonus points: If certain criteria apply (e.g. academic progress or number of ECTS credits obtained in the previous semester), students automatically receive additional points. Points of the previous semester: If you are on the waiting list of a course, you will automatically be credited the allocated points for the same course in the following semester and these will be added to your newly allocated points. # Student Space: room for students

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

© Alexander / Arnberger

© derknopfdruecker.com

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

selected.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
selected.text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

selected.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
selected.text:
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.
